speaker: TALBOT
text: Go to the gates of Bordeaux, trumpeter.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Summon their general unto the wall.

speaker: TALBOT
text: English John Talbot, captains, calls you forth,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Servant-in-arms to Harry, King of England,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And thus he would: open your city gates,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Be humble to us, call my sovereign yours,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And do him homage as obedient subjects,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And I’ll withdraw me and my bloody power.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But if you frown upon this proffered peace,

speaker: TALBOT
text: You tempt the fury of my three attendants,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Lean Famine, quartering Steel, and climbing Fire,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Who, in a moment, even with the earth

speaker: TALBOT
text: Shall lay your stately and air-braving towers,

speaker: TALBOT
text: If you forsake the offer of their love.

speaker: GENERAL
text: Thou ominous and fearful owl of death,

speaker: GENERAL
text: Our nation’s terror and their bloody scourge,

speaker: GENERAL
text: The period of thy tyranny approacheth.

speaker: GENERAL
text: On us thou canst not enter but by death;

speaker: GENERAL
text: For I protest we are well fortified

speaker: GENERAL
text: And strong enough to issue out and fight.

speaker: GENERAL
text: If thou retire, the Dauphin, well appointed,

speaker: GENERAL
text: Stands with the snares of war to tangle thee.

speaker: GENERAL
text: On either hand thee, there are squadrons pitched

speaker: GENERAL
text: To wall thee from the liberty of flight;

speaker: GENERAL
text: And no way canst thou turn thee for redress

speaker: GENERAL
text: But Death doth front thee with apparent spoil,

speaker: GENERAL
text: And pale Destruction meets thee in the face.

speaker: GENERAL
text: Ten thousand French have ta’en the Sacrament

speaker: GENERAL
text: To rive their dangerous artillery

speaker: GENERAL
text: Upon no Christian soul but English Talbot.

speaker: GENERAL
text: Lo, there thou stand’st, a breathing valiant man

speaker: GENERAL
text: Of an invincible unconquered spirit.

speaker: GENERAL
text: This is the latest glory of thy praise

speaker: GENERAL
text: That I, thy enemy, due thee withal;

speaker: GENERAL
text: For ere the glass that now begins to run

speaker: GENERAL
text: Finish the process of his sandy hour,

speaker: GENERAL
text: These eyes, that see thee now well-colorèd,

speaker: GENERAL
text: Shall see thee withered, bloody, pale, and dead.

speaker: GENERAL
text: Hark, hark, the Dauphin’s drum, a warning bell,

speaker: GENERAL
text: Sings heavy music to thy timorous soul,

speaker: GENERAL
text: And mine shall ring thy dire departure out.

speaker: TALBOT
text: He fables not; I hear the enemy.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Out, some light horsemen, and peruse their wings.

speaker: TALBOT
text: O, negligent and heedless discipline,

speaker: TALBOT
text: How are we parked and bounded in a pale,

speaker: TALBOT
text: A little herd of England’s timorous deer

speaker: TALBOT
text: Mazed with a yelping kennel of French curs.

speaker: TALBOT
text: If we be English deer, be then in blood,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Not rascal-like to fall down with a pinch,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But rather, moody-mad and desperate stags,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Turn on the bloody hounds with heads of steel

speaker: TALBOT
text: And make the cowards stand aloof at bay.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Sell every man his life as dear as mine

speaker: TALBOT
text: And they shall find dear deer of us, my friends.

speaker: TALBOT
text: God and Saint George, Talbot and England’s right,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Prosper our colors in this dangerous fight!
